entry.title: Issue Filtering Empty Fields in OData Analytical Model Calls
entry.content:
Hello,

Over the past few weeks, we have been experiencing issues with OData calls to analytical models when applying filters on empty fields.

We are building the filter, for example, as follows:
ZOFFICE ne ' '

Until recently, this expression worked correctly. However, it is no longer excluding records with empty values (' '). Instead, it returns the full result set, including those records where the field is empty.

Has there been any recent change in how empty values are interpreted or handled in OData filters? We have also tried alternatives such as using null or comparison operators like gt, but none of them have produced the expected behavior.

Thank you,
Jaume

entry.title: Mass update condition records amount
entry.content:
We are working on HANA 2000, for automobiles sector, not having Fiori. We have ZSFB condition type having country, customer in header and material, amount at item level. We have created LSMW but the user is asking for more simpler solution as he needs to upload 1000 records and each header contains 2000 materials in it.

Requirement is when the user maintains one customer with around 2000 materials, having all different prices, that time, all other customers should also get updated with those 2000 materials new price. The access sequence can't be changed as customer should be in header field.

entry.title: AI-Assisted SAP Datasphere Modeling
entry.content:
The Evolution of SAP Datasphere Modeling

Creating and managing SAP Datasphere models has traditionally been a manual, time-consuming process. However, with the integration of AI tools like Trae IDE, this process has been revolutionized, allowing data teams to focus on business logic rather than technical implementation details.

The Benefits of AI-Assisted Modeling

Efficiency and Speed

  • Rapid Model Generation: Create complex models in minutes instead of hours
  • Batch Operations: Generate multiple models simultaneously
  • Error Reduction: Eliminate manual JSON formatting and syntax errors
  • Consistent Output: Maintain uniform model structures across projects

Intelligent Design

  • Context-Aware Modeling: AI considers your existing data landscape
  • Optimal Structures: Designs models based on best practices
  • Relationship Mapping: Automatically identifies and creates data relationships
  • Scalable Architecture: Builds models that grow with your business needs

Accessibility

  • Lower Learning Curve: Reduces the technical expertise required
  • Plain Language Input: Describe requirements in natural language
  • Self-Documenting: Generates well-documented model definitions
  • Team Collaboration: Bridges the gap between business and IT teams

How It Works with Trae IDE

Trae IDE, an AI-powered development environment, simplifies SAP Datasphere modeling through:

  1. Project Analysis: Trae analyzes your existing codebase and data structures
  2. Intelligent Suggestions: Provides context-aware recommendations
  3. Automated Generation: Creates complete JSON model definitions
  4. Seamless Deployment: Facilitates easy deployment to your SAP Datasphere tenant

A Real-World Example

Let's look at a practical example from my repository:

Creating a User Information Table

  1. Define Requirements (the prompt can be replaced with the design document in an advanced version)

    Create a user information table with ID, name, email, and creation date.
Deploy to the SAP_SCT space.

  2. Generated JSON Definition

    {
  "definitions": {
    "USER_INFO_TABLE": {
      "kind": "entity",
      "elements": {
        "USER_ID": {
          "@EndUserText.label": "User ID",
          "type": "cds.Integer",
          "key": true,
          "notNull": true
        },
        "USER_NAME": {
          "@EndUserText.label": "User Name",
          "type": "cds.String",
          "length": 100,
          "notNull": true
        },
        "EMAIL": {
          "@EndUserText.label": "Email",
          "type": "cds.String",
          "length": 200,
          "notNull": true
        },
        "CREATED_DATE": {
          "@EndUserText.label": "Created Date",
          "type": "cds.Date",
          "notNull": true
        }
      },
      "@EndUserText.label": "User Information Table"
    }
  }
}

  3. Deployment Command

    datasphere objects local-tables create --file-path user-info-table.json --space SAP_SCT --host https://dwc-field-training.eu10.hcs.cloud.sap

  4. Verification

    {
  "message": "Saved and deployed 'USER_INFO_TABLE' in 'SAP_SCT'"
}

Integration with Modern Workflows

This approach seamlessly fits into contemporary data engineering practices:

  • CI/CD Pipelines: Automate model deployments
  • Version Control: Track changes to model definitions
  • Documentation: Generate self-documenting models
  • Testing: Validate models before production deployment

Getting Started

To implement this approach in your projects:

  1. Set Up Your Environment

    • Install @sap/datasphere-cli
    • Configure authentication to your SAP Datasphere tenant
    • Set up Trae IDE for AI-assisted development

  2. Define Your Requirements

    • Clearly articulate data model needs
    • Provide examples of existing models
    • Specify target spaces and naming conventions

  3. Leverage AI Assistance

    • Use Trae IDE to generate model definitions
    • Review and refine generated JSON
    • Test deployment in a development environment

  4. Deploy and Iterate

    • Deploy models to production
    • Monitor performance and usage
    • Iterate based on feedback

Join the Conversation

This article presents just one approach to AI-assisted SAP Datasphere modeling. We invite you to explore this technique and share your experiences.

  • Project Repository: https://github.com/yuyonggang/dsp-model-creator
  • Discussion: Feel free to open issues or pull requests with your ideas and improvements

Conclusion

AI-assisted modeling represents a significant advancement in SAP Datasphere development. By leveraging tools like Trae IDE, data teams can focus on business value while reducing technical overhead.

This approach not only accelerates model creation but also improves quality and consistency across your data landscape. As AI technology continues to evolve, we can expect even more powerful capabilities to emerge, further transforming how we work with SAP Datasphere.

Whether you're a seasoned data engineer or new to SAP Datasphere, this approach offers a more efficient, reliable way to build and manage your data models.

entry.title: Max Success Forums
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce Max Success Forums - a series of upcoming regional and local events designed to offer our customers a unique opportunity to receive firsthand insights from SAP executives and experts. The events will cover key topics such as Max Success Plan, agentic AI, SAP Business Data Cloud, enterprise architecture, integrated toolchain, and SAP Business Suite overall. Each event, spanning one or two days, offers a deep dive into critical topics covered at the Max Summit and highlights the central role success plans play for SAP's leading customers.

The events will feature customer success stories and breakout sessions led by SAP experts. Participants will enjoy networking opportunities and the chance to exchange experiences with fellow members of the Max community.
Please find below an overview of all currently announced regional and local Max Success Forums for each region. Updates will continuously be added to the respective pages.

EMEA

  • Spain: April 16 in Madrid
  • Netherlands: April 23 in ‘s-Hertogenbosch
  • Ireland: April 28 in Dublin
  • United Kingdom: April 30 in London
  • Italy: May 5 in Milan
  • Turkey: May 5 in Istanbul
  • France: June 4 in Paris
  • United Arab Emirates: June 18 in Dubai
  • Qatar: June 22 in Doha
  • Saudi Arabia: June 16 in Riyadh

We encourage you to register and reserve a spot in your calendar as soon as you receive your invitation.

We look forward to welcoming you to the regional and local events as we embark on a journey of transformation together. If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to share them in the comments below.

entry.title: Gregor Wolf
entry.content:
About Gregor

  • Germany | SAP Mentor since 2007 | Follow Gregor on LinkedIn

I am an independent SAP solution architect, developer and SAP Mentor. I focus on SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), Node.JS, SAP Fiori Elements and Security. I've supported several customers during their SAP Cloud ERP Private Edition (S/4HANA On Premise) implementation. Since the end of 2024 I support the technical topics at an SAP Cloud ERP Public Edition Customer.

Topics of interest: SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), SAP Cloud ERP public and private, SAPUI5, OpenUI5, SAP Fiori, SAP HANA Cloud

Gregor, what inspired you to become an SAP Mentor?

A key influence early in my SAP Community journey was Mark Finnern. He is the father of the SAP Community. Mark encouraged me to move beyond answering questions and start sharing detailed technical how-to's but also event reports in blog posts. In 2007 Mark also founded the SAP Mentors program which gave me the opportunity to provide structured, experience-based feedback to SAP product teams and help bridge the gap between product vision and real-world implementation.

What advice would like to share with other SAP community members?

My advice to other SAP Community members is simple: contribute consistently and think long-term.

Start by answering questions and helping others solve real problems - but don’t stop there. Document your lessons learned, share architectural insights, and write about what worked and what didn’t. That’s how collective expertise grows. And you will be amazed how often you research years later and find your own answers and blog posts to solve a problem you're just facing. Despite all the new stuff SAP has quite a long tail that doesn't change quickly.

Engage constructively, challenge respectfully, and focus on signal over noise. The community is not just a support forum - it’s an ecosystem where practitioners can influence product direction, elevate standards, and shape the future of SAP together.

entry.title: Re: How to call a BW ABAP-Backend from SAC Analytic Application
entry.content:
Hi, thank you very much for this blog, it has been really helpful.

I’m currently trying to reproduce this scenario (calling a BW process chain from SAP Analytics Cloud via an OData service), but I’m running into some issues and I’m not sure whether they are related to recent product changes.

Today, in our SAP Analytics Cloud tenant, it is no longer possible to create new Analytic Applications. SAC shows the message “Analytic applications are no longer supported” and recommends using the Optimized Story Experience instead.

My questions are:

  1. Is it still possible to implement the same approach described in this blog using Optimized Stories (with scripting and OData services), or does this scenario strictly require Analytic Applications?

  2. When trying to consume the OData service from an Optimized Story, the metadata does not load (no OData version, no actions are shown). Could this be a limitation of Optimized Stories, or is it more likely related to connectivity / CORS configuration?

  3. Is this approach still supported with SAP BW / BW/4HANA as the backend, or is it now only officially supported with SAP S/4HANA systems?

The OData service itself works correctly when tested from the backend (Gateway Client / browser), and the process chain can be started successfully. The issue only appears when trying to connect from SAC.

I’d really appreciate any clarification on whether this is still a supported scenario today, or if product changes in SAC have made this approach obsolete.

Thanks again for sharing your knowledge!

Hello all,

FYI: the system has to be reprovisioned every 24 months - as a *mandatory step*. According documentation there is no option to upgrade the system without full reprovisioning. This does not support the use case of building permanent demo cases and increases partner effort by a lot to recreate data to original state after a refresh.

Best regards

entry.title: Identifying and mitigating potential DDoS attacks in SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction:

DDoS and DoS attacks are often a cause of headaches for SAP Commerce Cloud admins, causing slowness and at a times even disruptions to the operation of the site. In this article we will show how to identify and use standard tools to mitigate the problems caused by these attacks.

Symptom 

It's important to be aware of symptoms of DDoS attacks, some of them listed below are very commonly observed in such situations:

  1. Apache worker pool exhausted:


  2. Abnormal high throughput:


  3. Apache Tomcat Thread pool exhaustion:


  4. High amount of logs like: [client xx.xx.xxx.xx:xxxxx] AH01998: Connection closed to child 262 with abortive shutdown (server _:443)

Screenshots above taken by OOTB SAP - Load Test v2 Dynatrace dashboard.

Cause

There are different types of cyber attacks that could be causing instability on the environment. Most common scenarios seen with SAP Commerce Cloud customers are:

  • DDoS (Distributed Denial of Service: it uses multiple machines mostly known as "bots" to flood a target server with traffic)
  • DoS (Denial of Service: uses a single machine to flood a target server with traffic)

With the SAP Commerce Cloud overloaded with fake requests the pods start to crash because traffic is retained and pilled up in reverse proxy Apache Ingress, exhausting the worker pool and causing several side effects.

Identification of DDoS/DoS

To start is necessary to check if there are abnormal number of requests coming from a single IP. The approach below is specially useful when attackers are using automatic scan requests approach to flood the site requests. To do it you can go to Dynatrace failure rate increase alert page that will usually come with the DoS/DDoS symptoms:

Select the impacted web service:

Go to view dynamic requests:

Select Failure Rate tab, then go to the unusual request with a high failure rate, click on the three dots and Service backtrace:

Click on the endpoint and select Client IPs tab:

If an abnormal number of requests coming from a single unusual IP is observed, it can be further validated by using an online free IP Lookup tool to get more information on the suspicious IP. You can correlate to a DoS in case the address is from an unusual location or a malicious IP. You can even check Blacklist Status to validate if the address is listed on most common security services.
*Note that this is not an useful approach with setups using a CDN on top of their Storefront, as the original IP will be overwritten by CDN IP. In this case the one will need to verify at CDN level what are the suspicious IPs.

Mitigation 

Web Application Firewall Lite: In case a DoS attack is identified and there's no CDN on top of Storefront, enabling WAF lite in Cloud Portal should relief the traffic, specially the option "Allowed Open Connections from A Single IP", values should be reviewed for every particular case and can be changed to fit different scenarios. For more information, please refer to Basic Web Application Firewall. For installations using CDN, the WAF should be adjusted at CDN level, which should give more tailored firewall options.

IP Filter Sets: In case a DDoS attack is identified and there's no CDN on top of Storefront, you can make use of IP filter sets to block the source machines flooding traffic. For reference, please check IP Filter Sets documentation and 3203549 - How to allow / deny access of IP addresses based on geolocation - SAP Commerce Cloud.

What is NOT a DoS or DDoS attack: 

In various scenarios, similar symptoms may be observed, but it is crucial to recognize that these occurrences do not necessarily indicate a cyber attack. It is necessary to collaborate with the customer to verify whether there are any ongoing sales seasons, load tests or surge events affecting the environment. Additionally, a more in-depth assessment can be conducted using Dynatrace and OpenSearch to verify the legitimacy of incoming requests.

entry.title: POs are not transferred to accruals engine for SES accruals
entry.content:
Dear Members
I have configured Purchase Order Accruals (POAC) functionality in SAP S/4HANA 2023 version and is working fine. I also configured SES Accruals (SSAC). Raised a PO (ME21N) with account assignment category K and Item category D with a limit. PO date 01.02.2026 and the delivery date is 30.04.2026. I raised a service entry sheet via tcode ML81N with posting date 20.02.2026. When I run ACEPOSTINGRUN (with date 28.02.2026) this PO is not getting captured. 
My questions are
  1. Should I raise the service entry sheet via Fiori App Manage Service Entry Sheets - Lean services (F2027) for SES accruals to trigger?
  2. If yes to the above, can I raise a SES via Fiori app F2027 against a PO raised via ME21N? 
Thanks
Kera

entry.title: Is there a dynamic form of "TYPES ... TYPE (structure) WITH INDICATORS ..."
entry.content:
I've got a code which is using "UPDATE zscarr FROM TABLE itab_zscarr INDICATORS SET STRUCTURE ind". ZSCARR is a copy of the table SCARR:

TYPES ty_zscarr TYPE zscarr WITH INDICATORS ind TYPE abap_bool.
DATA itab_zscarr TYPE TABLE OF ty_zscarr.
SELECT *
  FROM zscarr
	INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @itab_zscarr.
LOOP AT itab_zscarr REFERENCE INTO DATA(line_of_itab_zscarr).
  line_of_itab_zscarr->url = ''.
  line_of_itab_zscarr->ind-url = abap_true.
ENDLOOP.
UPDATE zscarr FROM TABLE itab_zscarr INDICATORS SET STRUCTURE ind.

The same code is to be done for other tables ZSCARR_2, ZSCARR_3... with the exact same structure.

I'd like to change the code to a dynamic form like this:

LOOP AT VALUE string_table( ( `ZSCARR` )
                            ( `ZSCARR_2` )
                            ( `ZSCARR_3` ) )
     INTO DATA(table_name).
  TYPES ty_zscarr TYPE (table_name) WITH INDICATORS ind TYPE abap_bool.
  DATA itab_zscarr TYPE REF TO DATA.
  CREATE DATA itab_zscarr TYPE TABLE OF (table_name).
  FIELD-SYMBOLS <itab_zscarr> TYPE TABLE.
  ASSIGN itab_zscarr->* TO <itab_zscarr>.
  SELECT *
    FROM (table_name)
    INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @<itab_zscarr>.
  LOOP AT <itab_zscarr> ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<line_of_itab_zscarr>).
    <line_of_itab_zscarr>-('URL') = ''.
    <line_of_itab_zscarr>-('IND-URL') = abap_true.
  ENDLOOP.
  UPDATE (table_name) FROM TABLE <itab_zscarr> INDICATORS SET STRUCTURE ind.
ENDLOOP.

All is fine except this obvious syntax error (TYPES has no dynamic form):

TYPES ty_zscarr TYPE (table_name) WITH INDICATORS ind TYPE abap_bool.

How to create the structured type WITH INDICATORS dynamically?

Thanks.

Sandra

NB: my system is S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 / ABAP 7.58 SP 1.

entry.title: Re: Is there a dynamic form of "TYPES ... TYPE (structure) WITH INDICATORS ..."
entry.content:
I propose below a helper class named ZCL_ABAP_RTTS to create a structured type WITH INDICATORS dynamically.

It compiles with ABAP 7.58 SP 1.

The class can be used as follows:

FIELD-SYMBOLS <itab_zscarr> TYPE STANDARD TABLE.

LOOP AT VALUE string_table( ( `ZSCARR` )
                            ( `ZSCARR_2` )
                            ( `ZSCARR_3` ) )
     INTO DATA(table_name).
  DATA(itab_zscarr) = zcl_abap_rtts=>get_table_with_indicators(
                            iv_structured_type_name      = table_name
                            iv_indicators_structure_name = 'IND' ##NO_TEXT
                            iv_indicator_type_char_1     = abap_true ).
  ASSIGN itab_zscarr->* TO <itab_zscarr>.

  SELECT *
    FROM (table_name)
    INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @<itab_zscarr>.

  LOOP AT <itab_zscarr> ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<line_of_itab_zscarr>).
    <line_of_itab_zscarr>-('URL') = ''.
    <line_of_itab_zscarr>-('IND-URL') = abap_true.
  ENDLOOP.

  UPDATE (table_name) FROM TABLE @<itab_zscarr> INDICATORS SET STRUCTURE ind.
ENDLOOP.

Code of class ZCL_ABAP_RTTS:

CLASS zcl_abap_rtts DEFINITION PUBLIC CREATE PRIVATE FINAL.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS get_table_with_indicators
      IMPORTING structured_type_name         TYPE csequence
                indicators_component_name    TYPE csequence         DEFAULT 'IND'
                indicator_type_char_1        TYPE abap_bool         DEFAULT abap_false
                table_kind                   TYPE abap_tablekind    DEFAULT cl_abap_tabledescr=>tablekind_std
                unique_key                   TYPE abap_bool         DEFAULT abap_false
                user_keys                    TYPE abap_keydescr_tab OPTIONAL
                key_kind                     TYPE abap_keydefkind   DEFAULT cl_abap_tabledescr=>keydefkind_default
      RETURNING VALUE(table_with_indicators) TYPE REF TO data.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS get_structure_with_indicators
      IMPORTING structured_type_name             TYPE csequence
                indicators_component_name        TYPE csequence
                indicator_type_char_1            TYPE abap_bool DEFAULT abap_false
      RETURNING VALUE(structure_with_indicators) TYPE REF TO data.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_abap_rtts IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_structure_with_indicators.
    TYPES ty_ref_to_cl_abap_elemdescr TYPE REF TO cl_abap_elemdescr.

    " Get the types of the components of the input structure
    DATA(rtts_of_structured_type) = CAST cl_abap_structdescr( cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_name( structured_type_name ) ).
    DATA(components_with_indicators) = rtts_of_structured_type->get_components( ).

    " Create the indicators structure
    DATA(indicator_components) = VALUE cl_abap_structdescr=>component_table(
        LET rtts_indicator_component_type = COND ty_ref_to_cl_abap_elemdescr( WHEN indicator_type_char_1 = abap_false
                                                                              THEN cl_abap_elemdescr=>get_x( 1 )
                                                                              ELSE cl_abap_elemdescr=>get_c( 1 ) )
        IN
            FOR <ls_component> IN components_with_indicators
            ( name = <ls_component>-name
              type = rtts_indicator_component_type ) ).
    DATA(rtts_indicators_structure) = cl_abap_structdescr=>get( p_components = indicator_components
                                                                p_strict     = abap_false ).

    " Add the indicators structure to the final structure WITH INDICATORS, as one structured component
    INSERT VALUE cl_abap_structdescr=>component( name = EXACT #( indicators_component_name )
                                                 type = rtts_indicators_structure )
           INTO TABLE components_with_indicators.

    " Create the data object corresponding to the final structure WITH INDICATORS
    DATA(rtts_structure_with_indicators) = cl_abap_structdescr=>get( p_components = components_with_indicators
                                                                     p_strict     = abap_false ).
    CREATE DATA structure_with_indicators TYPE HANDLE rtts_structure_with_indicators.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_table_with_indicators.
    DATA(structure_with_indicators) = get_structure_with_indicators(
                                          structured_type_name      = structured_type_name
                                          indicators_component_name = indicators_component_name
                                          indicator_type_char_1     = indicator_type_char_1 ).
    DATA(rtts_table_with_indicators) = cl_abap_tabledescr=>get(
        p_line_type  = CAST #( cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_data_ref( structure_with_indicators ) )
        p_table_kind = table_kind
        p_unique     = unique_key
        p_key        = user_keys
        p_key_kind   = key_kind ).
    CREATE DATA table_with_indicators TYPE HANDLE rtts_table_with_indicators.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Verify Journal Entries submission screen not showing tax data
entry.content:
When creating a journal entry via FIORI app: Verify General Journal Entries - for Requestor and submit it to the workflow, the display of the journal does not provide the tax line & tax information, which was entered during the journal creation.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Verify Journal Entries submission screen not showing...
entry.content:
When submitting a General Journal Document, the document doesn't get posted but parked. Because from a business process logic, parking is supposed to serve as a functionality when you save the document to process it later. During this process, taxes are not calculated at all (also many other checks are not done) because the document can be incomplete.

Please refer to the below KBA for more information.

KBA - ##2857275 - No tax line in the App Verify General Journal Entries - F2728 

This is the standard behaviour and the system is working as designed.

entry.title: Re: 2FA for ABAP systems
entry.content:
Hello AJP

From https://me.sap.com/notes/3320671 

From the Service provider side (for example fiori), the ABAP does not support 2FA "out of the box" for the SAML2 SSO.
 However, in case the IDP itself supports 2FA, it can be implemented there.
From the SAP side, the only 2FA implementation that exists relies in the SAP SSO 3.0 TOTPLoginModule, which can be installed on AS Java systems (One-Time Password Authentication: https://help.sap.com/doc/5e008ecd41234762a9d5a9c33b0ad6fe/3.0/en-US/One-TimePasswordAuthentication_UACP.pdf), where the system may act as an IDP.

entry.title: SAP GUI Automation Failing to Recognize Screen – Possible Impact of Windows Idle Session Timeout
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are currently experiencing an issue with one of our SAP GUI screen-based automations (SAP Build Process Automation / SAP GUI scripting).

The automation is able to:

  • Successfully log into Windows

  • Launch SAP GUI

  • Log into SAP

  • Navigate to the required transaction

  • Read data from SAP

However, after performing a period of background processing (Excel data handling), when the automation attempts to continue interacting with SAP GUI, it fails to recognize the expected screen.

At that moment, we observe the following popup message in SAP GUI:

“Idle timer expired. Session has been idle over its time limit. It will be disconnected in 2 minutes. Press any key now to continue session.”

Important observations:

  • SAP GUI does not immediately close.

  • The SAP Logon window remains open.

  • However, the automation is no longer able to recognize the screen elements.

  • The error typically appears as “Page not recognized” or “Element not found”.

We would like to confirm whether:

  1. A Windows Remote Desktop idle session timeout policy is active.

  2. Any recent security or infrastructure changes could be affecting GUI scripting sessions during periods of inactivity.

The automation is screen-based and depends on a stable SAP GUI session without modal popups or session interruptions.

Could you please confirm if the idle timeout policy (Windows or SAP level) may be impacting SAP GUI scripting and RPA execution?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: “Stage” Button Not Triggering Any Action in Stage for...
entry.content:
Dear @kkumara,

1. Why Stage looks like it does nothing:

A. No popup is expected

In this proces Stage is not a wizard. It’s an action on the selected replenishment element(s). Document creation is part of the staging process and is typically finalized when you Save and not by opening a follow-up screen. SAP Note for the app explicitly talks about pressing Stage and whether the save becomes enabled / whether the delivery is created

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2960232

B. In your screenshot you already staged everything

Top table:

  • Miss. qty = 6 / 21
  • Quantity staged = 6 / 21
  • Remaining = 0.000 for both lines

Bottom table:

  • Replenishment elements exist
  • Quantity staged already filled

So the system considers the shortages fully staged already pressing Stage again produces no visible change often no message. This matches the typical staging logic

2. What documents should be created:

To verify creation:

  • Check Manage Outbound Deliveries (VL06O) / EWM monitoring as per SAP guidance delivery should be visible and linked to staging
  • If you expect a delivery but don’t see one even though the app log claims it was created, SAP has a dedicated Public Cloud Note for that symptom

SAP Notes below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3354265

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3624697

3. Control test:

Do this controlled test:

  • Pick a case where Remaining > 0 a real shortage not yet staged or create a new requirement
  • In the Pull List select the bottom table (Replen. Elements) line(s) next Stage and Save
  • Then verify delivery creation in the delivery monitoring apps above

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2960232

Summary:

  • Nothing happens because there is nothing left to stage quantity staged fully covers Missing qty and Remaining is 0

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP Tax and Revenue Management - What is new for the S/4HANA 2025 FPS1 Release
entry.content:
Dear readers,

In a previous blog (SAP Tax and Revenue Management - What is new for the S/4HANA 2025 (initial) release I covered the new developments introduced in the S/4HANA 2025 FPS0 release. In this post, I will discuss what’s new in the S/4HANA 2025 FPS1 release:

  • Enable archiving of form data stored in the business document service (BDS/XML)

For Tax & Revenue Management, the archiving for form bundles was enhanced by the possibility to archive XML form data during the archiving run and delete the XML forms during the deletion run.

Compressed XML forms and alternative XML storage are supported as well.

The existing reload program was enhanced to handle reloading/re-creation of BDS documents for XML forms and restoring the DFMCA_FORM_DATA entries for XML forms.

This development has been downloaded all the way to ECC EhP8 (https://me.sap.com/notes/3648380)

For more information, see:

 

  • Disable the creation of new data form versions when updating existing form data

In legacy TRM, every change of formdata of an XML form lead to the creation of a new data version (corresponding to an extra line in dfmca_form_vers and an extra internal XML document).  This new development allows the prevention of the creation of new data versions via customizing.

The new development is relevant for all channels processing forms: standard UI processes, API usage (via FMCA_RETURN_UPDATE), or “upload” via Emigall (via FMCA_RETURN_UPDATE).

For more information, see:

 

  • Virtual Data Model for Tax and Revenue Management analytics

Virtual data model for analytics in tax and revenue management that provides streamlined and harmonized access to the tax and revenue management data model

Content that includes form bundle headers and form data for tax and revenue management analytics

For more information, see:

entry.title: Re: On production order release, two storage locations st...
entry.content:
Dear @SANDY13973,

In Public Cloud standard ATP/material availability check can be either:

  • Storage-location level checks only the issue/production SLoc in the requirement

or

  • Plant level checks across storage locations in the plant

The switch is in Configure Scope of Availability Check in CBC as SSCUI 101099

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3246520

 

Indicator: Without Storage Location Check

  • Set: ATP check is performed at plant level (so stock from multiple SLocs is included)
  • Not set: Only the production/issue storage location is considered

So the standard way to consider 2 SLoc stocks is to switch to plant-level check

IMPORTANT: Standard configuration does not provide sum stock from exactly two specific SLocs but exclude others. Plant-level means all storage locations of the plant are in scope. If you truly must limit to exactly 2 you typically solve it operationally for example keep stock only in those SLocs for that material or consolidate into a staging SLoc before release

To release production order only if components are available use option in CBC Define Checking Control SSCUI 102447. SAP confirms there are 3 release options when parts are missing:

 

1. User decides on release if parts are missing

2. Release permitted despite missing parts

3. No release if parts are missing

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3516522

https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-manufacturing-execution/configuring-material-availability-checks-for-production-orders

If you want the system to block release, use option 3 for your plant + order type entry

IMPORTANT: If you use background/mass processes/APIs the User decides … popup option is not handled because no dialog can be shown. SAP explicitly notes only option 2 & 3 are considered in API context

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3029979

Additional information:

Public Cloud uses Fiori apps/background jobs but the same SSCUI 102447 logic applies for collective release. SAP learning explicitly references collective release via apps like Manage Production Orders / Mass Processing Production Orders and background release via Schedule Order Release Run

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-manufacturing/releasing-production-orders-1

For mass availability checking you can also schedule background checks via Schedule Collective Availability Check for Orders

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/473277665900410b9e92ef9861ebb748.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/c21cf9fb0c514422a3875a96e9f74352.html

In CBC in SSCUI 102447 ensure you maintain:

  • the correct Plant + Order Type
  • the correct Checking Rule this checking rule + the material’s checking group determines which scope of check entry is used
  • and also align the Material availability check behavior at release for example Status check vs Check when saving to ensure missing parts status is correctly enforced during release.

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3686314

https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-manufacturing-execution/configuring-material-availability-checks-for-production-orders

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Pricing in Service PO according to service specification
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA, I am creating a Service PO (item category D) using a generic service material for example "Cutting".
The actual service price depends on specific service attributes (for example: thickness range, width range, weight bracket, grade, etc.).

If I maintain yearly contracts with pricing in SAP, the PO can pull the net price from the contract item.
However, for detailed pricing (e.g., multiple price components), I normally need to enter lines in the Service tab (ESLL) manually.

My question is:
Is there any SAP standard way for the system to automatically determine the correct service price and automatically populate the Service tab (ESLL) based on service attributes, without manual entry?

Specifically:

  • How can SAP “know” which price line applies?
  • Can service specifications drive automatic pricing in standard?
  • Is contract pricing with scales or conditions the only standard option?

entry.title: Re: More insights, faster resolution: Expanding Base Memory for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello, I can give an example from the Implementation perspective. If you have more than one SAP Cloud ALM system, important data ends up distributed in multiple places which limits your ability to re-use the data (for example, solution documentation, tasks, requirements, test cases, etc.) In a single SAP Cloud ALM, you can assign a document to multiple entities such as the process hierarchy, process diagrams, tasks, requirements, and more. However, you cannot easily browse, find and then assign objects from a different SAP Cloud ALM. It's possible to assign a document located in another SAP Cloud ALM by URL reference, but this only displays the header data and limits how it's searchable from another tenant. 

SAP is investigating methods of sharing information between SAP Cloud ALM tenants, but there is no details yet on availability. This question came up previously in the SAP Community post: How can we get one cloud alm tenant data into another cloud alm tenant and using which api? 

There is an exception in the case of Service Delivery information - customers now have the option to maintain their own mapping which service information should go into which SAP Cloud ALM tenant. See the SAP Community post: Transfer of Service Data for Customers entitled to several SAP Cloud ALM Tenants.

Product Group A has 5 Material IDs with the following shipment history:

Material IDHistorical Month 1Historical Month 2Historical Month 3Historical Month 4Historical Month 5Historical Month 6
Item 11121001039811294
Item 2839491889987
Item 3161169171150154178
Item 4257742
Item 59910611594103115

If we decide products 1,2,3, and 5 are phasing out and put end date in before the 4th item will phase out, the forecast aggregation sends ALL the remaining volume in item 4, as shown below.  This remaining item is not actually expected to increase, the volume will be moving to a different product family.  

Material IDForecast Month 1Forecast Month 2Forecast Month 3Forecast Month 4Forecast Month 5Forecast Month 6
Item 110700000
Item 2000000
Item 31641720000
Item 4199273448450460475
Item 5000000

Is there a way to stop the future volume from being shifted?  Or a way to mark a material so its volume is not referenced in the future for forecasting purposes?

Any ideas are greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Breaking SAP Data Barriers with Datasphere & Databricks: A Medallion Journey (Bronze, Silver, Gold)
entry.content:
IMPORTANT NOTE

SAP Datasphere does not replace Medallion architecture.
It simply shifts or enhances parts of your pipeline.

Think of it like this:

Datasphere = SAP-centric modeling + governed data access
Databricks = Open lakehouse + scalable transforms + AI/ML engine

Together, they complement each other.

Understanding the Medallion Architecture for SAP Data Layers

The Medallion approach structures data into layers, improving data quality as it moves upward.

🥉Bronze Layer – Raw SAP Data Landing Zone

This is where SAP data enters into Datasphere with Databricks.

What’s inside:

  • Integrated Raw data extracted from SAP ECC/S4 (tables, CDS views, BW objects)
  • Data in SAP-specific formats such as IDocs, ABAP extracts, or ODP streams
  • No transformations—just ingestion in its original state

Sources commonly used:

  • SAP ODP (Operational Data Provisioning)
  • SAP SLT (real‑time replication)
  • SAP BW extractors
  • SAP S/4HANA CDS Views
  • Files from SAP Application Servers

Goals of Bronze:

  • Ensure complete, unaltered SAP data
  • Maintain lineage to SAP tables
  • Enable incremental loading (Delta)
  • Provide a historical snapshot

Think of Bronze as your foundation of truth.

🥈Silver Layer – Cleaned & Harmonized SAP Data

This layer transforms raw SAP data into analytics‑ready tables.

What happens in Silver:

  • Data cleansing (null handling, type casting, deduplication)
  • SAP business logic harmonization
  • Joining related tables (e.g., EKKO + EKPO for Purchasing)
  • Enriching with master data (Material, Customer, Vendor, GL Accounts)
  • Converting SAP-specific formats (like NUMC, timestamps, currencies)

Results:

You get clean, standardized, and reusable data models.

Why Silver matters:

Example : SAP ERP tables are highly normalized and cryptic—full of codes like MATNR, WERKS, EBELN.
Silver makes them meaningful for downstream analytics.

🥇Gold Layer – Business-Ready SAP Analytics

This top layer contains curated, business-oriented datasets.

Examples:

  • Financial dashboards (GL, AR, AP)
  • CO-PA profitability models
  • Supply chain KPI models (OTIF, Inventory Aging)
  • Production analytics (yield, scrap, downtime)
  • Procurement insights (Spend Analytics)

Gold = Business Value

Here data is aggregated, enriched, and structured for:

  • BI tools (BW4, SAP Analytics Cloud)
  • ML models (forecasting, optimizations)
  • AI agents

It’s optimized for consumption, not technical storage.

The Medallion Architecture—Bronze, Silver, Gold—is the backbone for unleashing SAP data on Databricks.


LayerPurposeExamples
BronzeRaw SAP dataTables, IDocs, ODP extracts
SilverCleaned, harmonized SAP dataJoined purchasing docs, material data
GoldBusiness insightsFinance, supply chain, sales, procurement

 

Does Medallion Architecture Work with SAP Datasphere?

Yes — but it depends on how you use Datasphere.

The Medallion Architecture (Bronze → Silver → Gold) is a Databricks data engineering pattern.
SAP Datasphere is SAP’s cloud data fabric, which also has its own modeling layers (staging, semantic models, analytical models).

When Databricks and SAP Datasphere are connected, the Medallion Architecture still applies — but the roles can shift depending on your strategy.

Side-by-Side Comparison

ComponentSAP Datasphere RoleSAP Databricks Medallion Role
Raw data ingestionODP, SLT, SAP modelsBronze
Data cleansing & harmonizationOptional (Datasphere models)Silver
Business logic, KPIsOptional (Analytical models)Gold
Advanced AI/MLLimitedDatabricks ML/AI workspace
Scalable storageProprietary (SAP HANA Cloud)Open storage (Delta Lake)

entry.title: Text in GOS note popup not cleared after calling cl_gos_document_service->edit_note
entry.content:
I have implemented logic in a custom report to call the GOS popup for a specific note associated with a Business Partner in the customer role. The report displays a list of customers, with hotspots on each line item that allow the user to open the corresponding note.

If the customer does not yet have a note, the report calls cl_gos_document_service->create_note. If the note already exists, the report calls  cl_gos_document_service->edit_note.

The notes are successfully created and updated using these methods. However, an issue occurs after calling  cl_gos_document_service->edit_note: the previous text is not cleared from the popup. The text loaded during the first call to  cl_gos_document_service->edit_note continues to appear in all subsequent calls to both create_note and edit_note.

Does anyone know of a way to prevent this?

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting - Version Restrictions
entry.content:
Dear @FranConde,

In Public Cloud the SAP-recommended way is not to hide a consolidation version by catalogs but to control it via role restrictions / restriction types on the authorization field for Consolidation Version

In Public Cloud you maintain these checks via Business Roles + Restrictions in Maintain Business Roles and you use Display Restriction Types to see which catalogs support which restriction entities

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/9203905781b441ed9359cb29803f000a.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/8980ad05330b4585ab96a8e09cef4688.html

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/defining-restrictions-for-business-roles

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/c926d691d7144f7dba16f8e12ad81d28.html

Best-practice approach:

1. Start from SAP delivered Group Reporting role templates

SAP delivers Group Reporting templates/roles such as:

  • SAP_BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT_GRP, SAP_BR_GRP_ACCOUNTANT, SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR_GRP, SAP_BR_EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_GRP, SAP_BR_BUSINESS_ANALYST_GRP

These come with the Group Reporting scope content, SAP notes 1SG scope activation as a prerequisite for templates in some tenants

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3620561

Recommendation is to copy the needed templates to Z roles then maintain restrictions there upgrade-safe pattern

2. Restrict

In Maintain Business Roles:

a. Open the role

b. Go to Maintain Restrictions

c. For the Group Reporting catalogs in that role find the restriction type for:

    • Consolidation Version

d. Set access to Restricted and maintain allowed values so that A10 is NOT included

    • For example allow A00/A01/… but exclude A10

Do this for all Group Reporting roles that should not access A10 (Group Accountant, Analyst, etc.)

3. Don’t miss a catalog:

Use Display Restriction Types to:

  • search for RVERS / Consolidation Version
  • see which business catalogs use it
  • then confirm you maintained the restriction for those catalogs in the roles

This is important because users can otherwise still access A10 via another catalog/app path especially reporting vs processing

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/9203905781b441ed9359cb29803f000a.html

4. Validate with Display Authorization Trace

If a test user can still select/run A10 somewhere use Display Authorization Trace app to see which check/catalog granted it Public Cloud equivalent of troubleshooting missing/excess authorizations

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/b8c8128b86ae4e90b0ebb582d27a1980.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Understanding the Basic Terminologies of SAP Cloud World : A Beginner ‑ Friendly Guide
entry.content:
There are not enough fingers and toes to count all the statements that are inaccurate or plain wrong in this post. 

"You own/manage servers like ECC" - many ECC systems are also hosted and the customers neither own not "manage" servers. (Although it depends on what exactly you're calling "manage".)
"Full ABAP Freedom - Custom Code, Debug, SE80/ADT" - OK but this applies in Private Cloud too, so what's the point?

𝗣𝗿𝗶𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗖𝗹𝗼𝘂𝗱
"SAP Manages Servers for you" - If you're referring to RISE, technically SAP doesn't really manage any "servers" here, the hyperscalers do (same caveat about "manage"). Also, this can probably be done without SAP altogether. Fact-checking is something authors usually do.
"Limited Custom Code - Must Follow Clean Core Principles" - there is absolutely nothing enforcing "clean core". It is advisable for on-premise and ECC as well. It's not a distinguishing quality of Private Cloud system at all.

"No custom ABAP allowed" in Public Cloud is some next level bs (why did SAP bother coming up with ABAP Cloud then?). I could go on and on. 

Honestly, for the amount of misinformation in this post, the moderators should seriously consider removing this from SAP website.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite- Deep dive into Content Modifier
entry.content:
It's all in the error message

Method with name: replaceAll('X','') not found on bean: org.apache.camel.converter.stream.InputStreamCache

This means that the message body is treated as InputStreamCache and not java.lang.String when using Expressions in the Body section of a Content Modifier. Couple ways around this:

1. Use the properties tab, which uses String type by default.

/* Property Tab - Set to property, use the property in Body tab */
${body.replaceAll('X','')}

2. Skip the extra Property entirely and just cast the body to a String via bodyAs(<class>). This works in the Body/Property/Headers tabs, though I wouldn't do it as a header due to size limitations in headers.

${bodyAs(java.lang.String).replaceAll('X','')}

entry.title: Re: Issue with Deferred Revenue Posting in SAP ByDesign
entry.content:
Hello Gloria, 

A down payment received should not be revenue - it is just an advance payment from the customer.  First you must, from the sales order ideally, create the Down Payment Request and you can send it to the customer.  No journal entries are created at this point.   When the customer pays the Down Payment, and the cash is posted against the Down Payment Request, then there is a JE which debits cash, and credits the Accounts Receivable account that has been designated in the ADG determination activity.  The cash from the customer is not (nor should it be) deferred revenue.  When you create the subsequent customer invoice, the system will see the down payment and offer to net it against the invoice.   When you do a revenue recognition run, it looks at the rules you set up in Accrual Method Determination without regard to the existence of any down payment. 

Thanks, 

Carol Casey

Phoenix Business Consulting

entry.title: State central bank indicator in vendor master data?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Is there (in SAP S/4HANA 2023) any technical way to get the SCB indicator into a vendor's master in order to use it the same way we use payment terms or payment methods etc.? 

I stumbled across this with business partner master data, but there is no trace of an SCB indicator in the vendor master record in the company code (LFB1), but only in the document item (BSEG). From a purely functional point of view, however, this would be a great solution, as we usually always use the same SCB indicator for the same supplier. It would be great if it were then automatically transferred from the master record to the invoice documents.

Is this supported in any way?

Thank You and best regards

Rado

@GregBotticchio is SAP planning to add multiple variations of YtY calculations that would SAC handle within the same column? eg. I want to have column that will do YtY Better/ Worse comparison of 3 elements- Revenue, Gross Profit Margin and Controllable spend category (eg. Domestic labor) with the following logic
Revenue= (Current Year - Previous Year)/ Previous Year, result in %
Gross Profit Margin= (CY % - PY %)/ PY %, result in points
Domestic Labor= (PY - CY ) / PY, result in %
As far as I am aware, I am not able to have one B/(W) YtY column in SAC story that can handle all 3 different ways of calculation.

entry.title: Customer Specific Inventory Reservation based on Bill to party
entry.content:
Our client is seeking an SAP solution to implement a customer-based reservation (at the Bill-to party level) for specific products. 

  1. Reserved stock must be tracked separately from unrestricted inventory.
  2. Sales Orders for the designated customer must draw from the reserved stock first.
  3. Once the reserved stock is fully exhausted, any remaining order quantity should automatically be fulfilled from unrestricted stock.
  4. The reserved stock must remain unavailable to all other customers.
  5. The Customer reservation will have the expiry date, after expiry the reserved stock should move back to unrestricted stock.   

How can we achieve this specific allocation and consumption flow in SAP? We are in SAP ECC.

Thanks in advance.

hi @N1kh1l 

I need your help regarding this. I need to create a data action to validate the data and give the indicator (1,2,3,4)

condition:

If there are values from headcount and salary assumptions = 1

If there are no values from both headcount and salary assumptions = 2

If there are values from headcount but no values from salary assumptions = 3

If there are values from salary assumptions but no values from headcount = 4

and here is my logic :

IF RESULTLOOKUP(
[d/Measures] = "HEADCOUNT",
[d/DIM_MANPOWER_BUDGET_ITEM] = "A5000_001",
[d/DIM_CURRENCY] = "#"

RESULTLOOKUP(

) = 2
ENDIF
 
// Need Assumption Indicator for Special Payment Assumptions
IF RESULTLOOKUP(

[d/DIM_MANPOWER_BUDGET_ITEM] = "A5000_001",
[d/DIM_CURRENCY] = "#"

// Need Headcount Indicator for Special Payment Assumptions

entry.title: Re: Custom Cube for Margin Analysis - Calculated Key Figure shows no data
entry.content:
If the calculated measure shows values in ADT data preview but not in Analysis Office or other BW tools, the issue is likely in the BI semantics layer (exception aggregation, currency handling, or query variables).

If the calculated measure is already blank in ADT preview, then the issue is in the CDS modeling. In that case, verify aggregation, currency semantics, and whether the measure is properly propagated when one cube consumes another. Calculated measures defined in a lower cube are not automatically exposed or re-annotated in higher-level cubes.  

Thanks, 

Koushik G

entry.title: SAP IoT Edge to SAP CPI Integration – Enterprise Architecture Explained
entry.content:
In today’s industrial landscape, organizations are rapidly adopting IoT-enabled solutions to collect real-time data from machines, sensors, and PLCs. However, collecting device data is only the first step — the real value comes from integrating this data into enterprise systems like ERP, analytics platforms, and business applications.

This is where SAP IoT Edge, SAP IoT, and SAP Cloud Platform Integration work together to create a scalable, secure, and enterprise-ready IoT architecture.

What is SAP IoT Edge ?
1) It is a Edge-side software installed near machines (factory / plant / gateway).
2) Collects data from Sensors, PLCs, and industrial machines.
3) Supports OPC-UA, MQTT, Modbus, REST Adapters.
4) Performs local filtering, aggregation, buffering.
5) Works with low-latency and unstable internet.

SAP IoT Edge – Role in Integration
1) Direct interface to physical devices.
2) Handles industrial protocols not supported by CPI.
3) Reduces data volume before cloud transmission.
4) Ensures reliability and near real-time processing.

What is SAP IoT (BTP Services)?

1) SAP IOT BTP is a Cloud-based IoT services running on SAP BTP.
2) Central IoT platform for device onboarding and management.
3) Provides rules, events and APIs.
4) Acts as a secure IoT backbone for SAP landscape.

SAP IoT (BTP) – Role in Integration

1) Manages device lifecycle and security.
2) Processes and enriches IoT data in the cloud.
3) Triggers rules and events.
4) Exposes data via APIs/events for SAP CPI consumption.

What is SAP CPI?

1) SAP Cloud Platform Integration is a middleware.
2) Integrates SAP and non-SAP applications.
3) Enterprise-grade integration, monitoring, and governance.
4) Does NOT communicate directly with sensors or PLCs.

SAP CPI – Role in Integration

1) Consumes APIs/events from SAP IoT (BTP).
2) Performs mapping, routing, validation, enrichment.
3) Handles error handling, retries, and monitoring.
4) Integrates with S/4HANA, ECC, Analytics, Non-SAP apps.

--> Now we will see the what are the usable adapters for the all three integration aspects .

Usable Adapters – SAP IoT Edge

1) OPC-UA (Open Platform Communications Unified Architecture) –> Industrial machines and PLCs.
2) MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport ) –> Lightweight device messaging.
3) Modbus –> Legacy industrial devices.
4) REST / HTTP –> Smart devices and gateways.

Usable Adapters – SAP IoT (BTP)

MQTT –> Secure device data ingestion
REST / HTTP – > API-based access to IoT data
Event APIs – > Event-driven processing and notifications

Usable Adapters – SAP CPI

HTTP / REST –> Primary IoT integration adapter
OData –> SAP S/4HANA APIs
SOAP –> Legacy web services
RFC / IDoc –> SAP ECC / On-Prem integration

Recommended Architecture (Enterprise Ready/High Volume)

-->ow we going through the Architecture of the integration between SAP IOT EDGE and SAP CPI.

Architecture -1

 Alternative Architecture (PoC / Low Volume)

-->Now is the alternative for the above , it will be used only for the POC's point of view like for the low volumes of data from the IOT devices.

Architecture -2

--> CPI is cloud-only and not suitable for high-volume, real-time device data.
--> It also lacks device management capabilities.
--> Direct integration should be limited to PoC or very low-volume scenarios.

IFLOW DEMO

DEMO IFLOW

 -->So, Now we are having a iflow demo here.

-->In this demo I have taken the postman to post the json data with temperatures and first it will convert the json data to xml data.

--> After the conversion, the xml data is routed based on the conditions we provided like when the temperatures are greater than or equal to  90 degrees Centigrade it will be moved to content modifier and the data is modified for the mail payload and sent the alert to the team.(In this I haven't connected mail).

-->When normal temperatures comes , then the data will be moved to default route.

Key Takeaways

1) IoT Edge  = Near the machine.
2) SAP IoT (BTP)  = Brain in the cloud.
3) SAP CPI = Messenger to business systems.
4) Using SAP IoT (BTP) in between makes the solution more stable, secure, and enterprise-ready.

Conclusion

A robust IoT integration landscape requires clear separation of responsibilities across layers. SAP IoT Edge manages device-level communication and industrial protocols near the machines, SAP Internet of Things on SAP BTP handles cloud processing, device management, and event exposure, and SAP Cloud Platform Integration integrates IoT data into enterprise systems like SAP S/4HANA.

For enterprise and high-volume scenarios, using SAP IoT (BTP) between IoT Edge and CPI ensures scalability, security, and stability. Direct integration should be reserved for PoC or low-volume use cases.

entry.title: Re: AND function in SAC is not working
entry.content:
@Aliyah_Mazaya67 

With limited dimensionality information of your model, Your condition test looks fine so I do not think there is an issue with AND conditions. What I can see is all you conditions when TRUE they write to the same intersection.

 

DATA([d/Measures] = "VALIDATION",[d/DIM_MANPOWER_BUDGET_ITEM] = "A5000_205",[d/DIM_CURRENCY] = "#",[d/Date] =[d/Version].[p/M1Y1]) = 1
 
DATA([d/Measures] = "VALIDATION",[d/DIM_MANPOWER_BUDGET_ITEM] = "A5000_205",[d/DIM_CURRENCY] = "#",[d/Date] =[d/Version].[p/M1Y1]) = 2
 
DATA([d/Measures] = "VALIDATION",[d/DIM_MANPOWER_BUDGET_ITEM] = "A5000_205",[d/DIM_CURRENCY] = "#",[d/Date] =[d/Version].[p/M1Y1]) = 3
 
DATA([d/Measures] = "VALIDATION",[d/DIM_MANPOWER_BUDGET_ITEM] = "A5000_205",[d/DIM_CURRENCY] = "#",[d/Date] =[d/Version].[p/M1Y1]) = 4
 
 
Unless there is another dimension which differentiates over the test conditions like EMPLOYEE or EMPLOYEE group  you Validations will overwrite each other. The reason you see only 4 is due the reason that it is the last DATA() statement in order of writing and the result overwrites all previous results as the intersection is same.
 
Hope this helps !
Nikhil

entry.title: 🤖⚡ Meet Traceability Agent, my first custom Joule Agent for SAP Digital Manufacturing. ⚡🤖
entry.content:
Introduction

This is not a dashboard.
This is not a report.
This is an AI Agent that understands your manufacturing history instantly.

Traceability Agent is an expert in as-built and batch traceability, capable of answering complex questions in seconds:

🔎Top-down: What went into this SFC, batch, or assembly?
🔁Bottom-up: Where was this batch, material, or component used?
⚡Instant analysis across orders, SFCs, batches, and inventory
🧠 No manual investigation. No digging. Just ask.

What used to take minutes or hours… now takes seconds.
This is a massive leap toward AI-driven manufacturing intelligence, where Joule Agents become true digital experts embedded directly into SAP Digital Manufacturing.

What are Joule Agents?

Joule Agents are specialized, generative AI-powered, and collaborative agents from SAP designed to automate complex, non-deterministic business workflows across finance, supply chain, procurement, and HR. They function as intelligent, context-aware teammates that use SAP Knowledge Graph and business data to proactively resolve multi-step tasks, interact with systems, and make decisions.

Learn more: Joule Agents: How SAP Uniquely Delivers AI Agents That Truly Mean Business
Demo: Joule Agents: Dispute Resolution Agent

Key aspects of Joule agents include:

  • Context-Aware Automation: Unlike generic AI, Joule agents are deeply integrated with SAP data, understanding how actions in one area, such as the supply chain, affect others, like finance.
  • Proactive Action: These agents can operate independently in the background, identifying exceptions, sending alerts, and preparing recommendations without constant user prompting.
  • Collaboration: Joule agents can work together, with some agents calling upon others to solve, for example, a complex, multi-functional project that requires both procurement and logistics expertise.
  • Customization: Using Joule Studio's agent builder, companies can build, test, and deploy their own custom agents to meet specific needs if pre-built options are unavailable.
  • Use Cases: Examples include the ExpenseIt Agent for analyzing receipts, Career and Talent Development Agents for HR planning, and Meeting Location Planner Agents in SAP Concur.

Joule agents are accessed through conversational interfaces, acting as an extension of the broader Joule AI assistant.

Learn more about Joule Studio, Agent Builder, and take Joule Studio Product Tour.

How I built Traceability Agent?

Joule Agents can easily leverage Joule Skills out-of-the-box as building blocks (Tools) to retrieve data and perform actions. However, for Traceability Agent, I wanted to try something new. For the first time, I wanted to explore MCP Servers and how that could enhance or improve not only my overall build flow, but also the overall outcome of the agent.

So, I ended up with the following architecture:

Where I deployed a MCP Server in SAP BTP:

That connects to SAP Digital Manufacturing HANA Database directly:

Which gives the access my Joule Agent would need to retrieve SAP Digital Manufacturing data.

With that in place, I was able to start building agents using Joule Studio.

First, defining Name, Description, Expertise and Instructions:

Then, defining my preferred LLM Model Settings, which in my case I used Google Gemini 2.5 Pro:

Then, adding the MCP Server mapping:

And adjusting my output preferences:

So I could then Test it, Release it and Deploy it.

So what!? What this Agent is capable of?

Glad you asked! 🙂 This agent can do really cool as-built and traceability analysis, including:

🔎Top-down: What went into this SFC, batch, or assembly?

🔁Bottom-up: Where was this batch, material, or component used?

🔝Top and Ranking Batches

and much more!

Now, I´m working on Nonconformance, Rework, OEE and Labor Agents, so stay tuned.

Conclusion

Traceability Agent shows how manufacturing intelligence is evolving from manual investigation to instant, conversational insight.

What once required navigating multiple screens and reports can now be answered in seconds by an AI Agent that understands your SAP Digital Manufacturing data and context. By combining Joule Agents, Joule Studio, and MCP Servers, this approach enables AI-powered experts that can analyze traceability, identify relationships, and deliver answers on demand.

 

What do you think about custom Joule Agents for SAP Digital Manufacturing? Let me know in the comments!

Stay tuned for more! Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Inspection Lot Quantity field's value is not printing in Quality Certificate
entry.content:
We have developed a Quality Certificate for batches and mapped the Inspection Lot Quantity field using binding. Previously, the field value was printed successfully. However, for the past 3 to 4 months, we have observed that the Inspection Lot Quantity field value is no longer printing in the Quality Certificate for batches.

A similar issue occurred with the Inspection Date field. Therefore, we created a support case with SAP. After analyzing the Quality Certificate form, SAP proposed a solution to correct the binding and add the field logic in BADI QM_QCERTIFICATE_OUT_MODIFY.

After implementing the suggested changes, the Inspection Date field is now printing successfully. However, the Inspection Lot Quantity field value is still not printing.

We have tried multiple combinations and approaches, but we are still unable to print the Inspection Lot Quantity field value.

Could anyone please help us resolve this issue?

entry.title: Communication Arrangements in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Communication Arrangements

Communication Arrangements is one of the Key User Extensibility Tools.

With Communication Arrangements App,

  • Communication Arrangements details can be viewed.
  • Custom Communication Arrangements can be created with standard communication or custom communication scenarios using Communication Arrangements App.

Finding the Standard Communication Scenarios in SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Navigate to SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Choose the Product

SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition

Navigate to APIs -> ODATA V2 / ODATA V4.

Search Required API

List of results for Purchase Order.

Purchase Order API Overview

API Resources

Navigate to API Resources -> Attributes -> Communication Scenario

API Specifications

Authentication Methods for API

Configuration Details for API

Extensibility

Custom Fields can be added to the API using below Business Contexts - Header and Items in Custom Fields App with Key User Extensibility.

API Reference

List of Entity Sets Available in the APIs.

Communication Scenarios

Navigate to Display Communication Scenarios,

All these are Standard Communication Scenarios.

Standard Communication Scenarios of Purchase Order - SAP_COM_0053

Steps to Create Communication Arrangements

Maintain Communication Users

Navigate to Maintain Communication Users App.

Maintain Communication Users is to create Communication Users for the API Access.

Choose New button to create Communication User

Add User Name and Password.

Propose Password can be chosen to generate password by system.

Save the Communication User.

If communication User is added to any Communication system or communication Arrangements, here it shows those details.

Communication System

Navigate to Communication System App.

Communication System is to maintain communication system with host name and communication User.

Provide Communication System ID and Name.

Add the Host Name

Add necessary details

Choose Add button to add Inbound Communication User.

Available Authentication Methods

Required details for SSL Client Certificate.

Required details for OAuth 2.0

Choose the Authentication Method, and choose User Name/Client ID.

Click on Ok after choosing Communication User.

Communication User is added.

 Communication System is saved.

Communication Arrangements

Navigate to Communication Arrangements app.

Communication Arrangements is to generate Inbound Service URLs by adding Communication System and Communication User.

Choose New Option to create Custom Communication Arrangements.

Standard or Custom Communication Scenarios is required to create Custom Communication Arrangements.

Search for the Standard or Custom Communication Scenario

‘SAP_COM_0053’ is the Standard Communication Scenario for the Purchase Order API.

Arrangement appears automatically based on Communication Scenario and it can be changed.

Click on Create button to create Communication Arrangement.

List of Inbound Services available for that Communication Scenario.

WSDL/Service Metadata will be generated once Saved the Communication Arrangement.

Communication System and Communication User is required to create Communication Arrangements.

WSDL/Service Metadata is generated.

Service Metadata

Service URL is available in Communication Arrangement.

Access the Service URLs through the Communication User details.

Conclusion

standard communication or custom communication scenarios are used to create Communication Arrangements.

In Communication Arrangements, OData URLs are generated based on inbound Service.

References

  • Communication Arrangements | SAP Help Portal
  • Maintain Communication Users | SAP Help Portal
  • Communication Systems | SAP Help Portal
  • Integration | SAP Help Portal
  • Extend and Integrate Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal
